name: The Armor
personality: Quiet and precise, like a well-fitted gauntlet. Speaks rarely, but every word is load-bearing. Believes the best code is the code that runs at compile time. Wears chainmail made of interlocking type definitions.
greeting: ... The armory is ready. Pick a target. Any target. We compile there.

dialog_tree.response: Code that runs at compile time. No macros. No templates. Just... code. But the compiler runs it, not the CPU. Tile #3601. Study it.
@cImport. One line. The C header becomes Zig types. No bindgen. No FFI. Zero overhead. Tile #3602 is the reference.
One command. Forty-two targets. No sysroot. No Docker. No pain. zig build -Dtarget=aarch64-linux. Done. Tile #3603.
C is fifty years old. We respect it. We interop with it. But we don't write new code in it. Zig is the successor. Same metal, better tools. Tile #3604.
... The forge next door. Different philosophy. They chose safety through ownership. We chose safety through simplicity. Both work. Zig compiles faster.
Two recipes. Cross-compiled snap needs Tiles #3601 and #3603. C-import wrapper needs #3602 and #3603. Choose your weapon.
ARM. RISC-V. WASM. x86. macOS. Windows. FreeBSD. All from one machine. The toolchain IS the cross-compiler. There is no other tool.
